markdown.markdown:
This page showcases my hands-on experience using data analytics, visualization, and strategic insight to solve real-world marketing problems. ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## MEin3D — AI-Powered Email Personalization (Graduate Capstone) **From Clicks to Conversions: AI Powered Email Personalization Strategies for MEin3D** # AI-Personalized Email Marketing & Consumer Trust **Culminating Experience Project (CEP)** — Graduate research investigating whether AI-personalized emails generate higher consumer trust compared to standard emails, using an experimental survey design. - 🎯 **Focus:** AI email personalization, consumer trust, and email marketing effectiveness - 📊 **Tools Used:** R, Qualtrics, Excel - 🔬 **Methodology:** Experimental design (A/B test), One-Way ANOVA, Tukey HSD post-hoc test, Likert-scale survey (10 trust items) - 📈 **Outcome:** AI-personalized emails scored slightly higher in trust (M = 4.24) versus control (M = 4.16), but the difference was not statistically significant (F = 0.745, p = 0.39), suggesting trust levels are comparably high across both conditions. ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ### 📊 Key Findings **Average Trust Score by Condition** ![Bar chart showing AI condition (4.24) vs Control condition (4.16) average trust scores](images/ai-trust-bar.png) **95% Tukey Confidence Interval** ![Tukey confidence interval plot showing the difference in means crosses zero, indicating no significant difference](images/tukey-ci.png) **Summary Statistics Table** | Statistic | Value | |----------------------|--------------------------------------------------| | Mean Trust (AI) | 4.24 | | Mean Trust (Control) | 4.16 | | Mean Difference | 0.08 | | F-Statistic | 0.745 | | p-Value | 0.39 | | 95% CI (Tukey) | \[-0.280, 0.110\] | | Tukey p-Value | 0.39 | | Interpretation | No statistically significant difference in trust | **Trust Item Breakdown by Condition** ![Horizontal bar chart comparing AI vs Control scores across all 10 trust survey items](images/trust-breakdown.png%20.png) ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ### 💡 Insights - Both AI and control emails generated **high trust scores** (above 4.0 on a 5-point scale), suggesting the email content itself was well-received regardless of personalization. - The AI condition scored higher on **data comfort** and **accuracy/reliability**, while the control scored slightly higher on **privacy respect** and **authenticity**. - The lack of statistical significance may reflect a **ceiling effect** — participants rated both emails highly, leaving little room to detect meaningful differences. ## Future research could explore trust differences with **larger, more diverse samples** or test emails with stronger AI personalization cues. ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## MEin3D — AI-Powered Email Personalization (Graduate Capstone)

Use arrow keys or click the arrows to navigate · Click the expand icon for fullscreen

--- title: "From Clicks to Conversions" subtitle: "AI Powered Email Personalization Strategies for MEin3D" author: "Nerina Campos · Jessica Garcia · Kathleen Juarez · Krystal Lee · Serena Shi (Project Lead) | Faculty Lead: Dr. Elif Ozkaya" format: revealjs: theme: default transition: slide slide-number: true footer: "MEin3D | IBM 6530 | Group 2" width: 1280 height: 720 css: mein3d-slides.css auto-scale: true smaller: true scrollable: true execute: echo: false warning: false message: false --- ```{r setup, include=FALSE} library(tidyverse) library(knitr) library(kableExtra) library(ggplot2) library(tidyr) ``` ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## Business Problem & Project Overview :::::: columns :::: {.column width="48%"} ::: red-box **MEin3D** — small startup specializing in custom 3D-printed figurines for life's milestones. MEin3D lacked **automated, personalized, data-driven communication** — limiting trust, engagement, and long-term customer value. ::: :::: ::: {.column width="52%"} | AO | Question | Method | |-----|-------------------------------|-----------------| | AO1 | Non-personalized baseline? | Funnel analysis | | AO2 | Does AI affect trust? | One-way ANOVA | | AO3 | Social behavior → engagement? | A/B test | | AO4 | Which CTA drives lift? | Z-test | | AO5 | Where do users drop off? | GA4 funnel | ::: :::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## AO1 — Non-Personalized Baseline *Jessica Garcia* :::::: columns ::: {.column width="50%"} ```{r ao1-chart, fig.height=4} tibble( stage = factor(c("Sent","Delivered","Opened","Clicked"), levels=c("Sent","Delivered","Opened","Clicked")), n = c(77, 74, 20, 2) ) |> ggplot(aes(x=stage, y=n)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", fill="#666666", width=0.6) + geom_text(aes(label=n), vjust=-0.4, fontface="bold", size=6) + scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,90)) + labs(title="Baseline Email Funnel — March 10", x=NULL, y="Recipients") + theme_minimal(base_size=14) ``` ::: :::: {.column width="50%"} | Metric | Value | |--------------------|-----------| | Deliverability | **96.1%** | | Open Rate | **27.3%** | | Click-to-Open Rate | **10.0%** | | Unsubscribes | **0** | ::: red-box **Key finding:** 90% drop-off between open and click. The content and CTA are the bottleneck — not the subject line. This directly motivates AO4. ::: :::: :::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## AO2 — AI Personalization & Trust *Krystal Lee* :::::: columns ::: {.column width="52%"} ```{r ao2-chart, fig.height=4.2} tibble(condition=c("AI","Control"), mean_trust=c(4.24,4.16)) |> ggplot(aes(x=condition, y=mean_trust, fill=condition)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.5) + geom_text(aes(label=mean_trust), vjust=-0.5, fontface="bold", size=7) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("AI"="#777777","Control"="#aaaaaa")) + scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,5)) + labs(title="Average Trust Score by Condition", subtitle="10-item Likert scale (1–5) · n = 130", x=NULL, y="Score (1–5)") + theme_minimal(base_size=14) + theme(legend.position="none") ``` ::: :::: {.column width="48%"} | Statistic | Value | |----------------------|-----------------------| | Mean Trust (AI) | **4.24** | | Mean Trust (Control) | **4.16** | | F-Statistic | **0.745** | | p-Value | **0.39** | | 95% CI (Tukey) | **\[−0.280, 0.110\]** | ::: red-box AI personalization does **not** erode trust — **green light to scale personalized campaigns.** ::: :::: :::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## AO3 — Social Behavior & Engagement *Serena Shi* **Meta signals (likes, shares, saves, comments) used to classify subscribers → personalized vs. generic** ```{r ao3-table} library(knitr) library(kableExtra) library(tibble) library(dplyr) tibble( email_type = c("Personalized", "Generic"), subject_line = c("Your Car Could Be Next", "Something New Is Coming This April"), Sent = c(73, 74), Opened = c(19, 16), Clicked = c(4, 0), open_rate = c("26%", "22%"), click_rate = c("5%", "0%") ) |> rename( "Email Type" = email_type, "Subject Line" = subject_line, "Open Rate" = open_rate, "Click Rate" = click_rate ) |> kable(escape = FALSE) |> kable_styling(full_width = TRUE, font_size = 20) |> row_spec(1, background = "#fdecea", bold = TRUE) ``` ::: red-box **Personalization drives action** — 5% click rate vs. 0% for generic. Behavior-based messaging creates stronger connection and higher conversion potential. ::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## AO4 — CTA Feature Optimization *Nerina Campos* ```{r ao4-chart, fig.height=3.6, fig.width=11} tibble( metric=factor(rep(c("Open Rate (%)","CTR (%)","CTOR (%)"),each=2), levels=c("Open Rate (%)","CTR (%)","CTOR (%)")), condition=rep(c("Generic","Personalized"),3), value=c(23.1,29.9,1.38,3.56,5.6,11.4) ) |> ggplot(aes(x=condition, y=value, fill=condition)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.55) + geom_text(aes(label=paste0(value,"%")), vjust=-0.4, fontface="bold", size=5.5) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("Generic"="#888888","Personalized"="#c0392b")) + facet_wrap(~metric, scales="free_y") + labs(title="Personalized CTA vs. Generic — 5 Campaign Rounds", x=NULL, y=NULL) + theme_minimal(base_size=14) + theme(legend.position="bottom", strip.text=element_text(face="bold",size=13)) ``` ::::::::: columns :::: {.column width="33%"} ::: stat-box-dark Open Rate **+6.7pp**\ 23.1% → 29.9% ::: :::: :::: {.column width="33%"} ::: stat-box-dark CTR **+2.6×**\ 1.38% → 3.56% ::: :::: :::: {.column width="33%"} ::: hero-metric CTOR **+2×**\ 5.6% → 11.4%\ Z = −1.90, p = 0.058 ::: :::: ::::::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## AO5 — Website Funnel Analysis *Kathleen Juarez* :::::: columns ::: {.column width="50%"} ```{r ao5-funnel, fig.height=4.2} tibble( stage=factor(c("Sessions","Page Views","Form Start","Bookings"), levels=c("Sessions","Page Views","Form Start","Bookings")), n=c(563,470,38,5), fill=c("a","a","b","c") ) |> ggplot(aes(x=stage, y=n, fill=fill)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + geom_text(aes(label=n), vjust=-0.4, fontface="bold", size=7) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("a"="#1a3a5c","b"="#c0392b","c"="#27ae60")) + scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,640)) + labs(title="Booking Funnel — Jan 1 to Apr 21, 2026", x=NULL, y="Users") + theme_minimal(base_size=14) + theme(legend.position="none") ``` ::: :::: {.column width="50%"} | Channel | Sessions | Conv. Rate | |----------------|----------|------------| | Unassigned | 32 | **3.1%** | | Organic Search | 86 | **2.3%** | | Organic Social | 207 | 0.5% | | Direct | 213 | 0.5% | | **TOTAL** | **563** | **0.9%** | ::: red-box **99.1% of visitors leave without booking.** Sharpest drop at Form Start. Organic Search converts 4× better than Social. ::: :::: :::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## Recommendations & Summary :::::::::::: columns ::::::::: {.column width="50%"} **Immediate (0–30 days)** ::: rec-item Deploy AI-personalized CTAs — 2.6× CTR, trust preserved ::: ::: rec-item Fix the booking form — 92% drop-off at Form Start ::: **Medium-Term (30–90 days)** ::: rec-item Invest in Organic Search — highest converting channel ::: ::: rec-item Scale A/B testing to n ≈ 600+ to confirm AO4 ::: **Long-Term (90+ days)** ::: rec-item Build automated email nurture sequences ::: ::: rec-item Implement GA4 purchase event tracking ::: ::::::::: :::: {.column width="50%"} | AO | Key Finding | |-----|-------------------------------------------| | AO1 | 10% CTOR baseline — content is bottleneck | | AO2 | AI preserves trust (p = 0.39) | | AO3 | 5% click vs. 0% generic | | AO4 | **2× CTOR, 2.6× CTR** lift | | AO5 | 99.1% don't book — fix funnel | ::: big-quote The trust is there. The lift is real. Fix the funnel. ::: :::: :::::::::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## Thank You ::: thank-you-box **Group 2 — IBM 6530** Nerina Campos · Jessica Garcia · Kathleen Juarez · Krystal Lee · **Serena Shi (Project Lead)** *Faculty Lead: Dr. Elif Ozkaya* GitHub: [github.com/soserena/mein3d_project](https://github.com/soserena/mein3d_project) :::

# 📊 Marketing Analytics Projects This page showcases my hands-on experience using data analytics, visualization, and strategic insight to solve real-world marketing problems. ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## MEin3D — AI-Powered Email Personalization (Graduate Capstone) **From Clicks to Conversions: AI Powered Email Personalization Strategies for MEin3D** # AI-Personalized Email Marketing & Consumer Trust **Culminating Experience Project (CEP)** — Graduate research investigating whether AI-personalized emails generate higher consumer trust compared to standard emails, using an experimental survey design. - 📈 **Outcome:** AI-personalized emails scored slightly higher in trust (M = 4.24) versus control (M = 4.16), but the difference was not statistically significant (F = 0.745, p = 0.39), suggesting trust levels are comparably high across both conditions. ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ### 📊 Key Findings **Average Trust Score by Condition** ![Bar chart showing AI condition (4.24) vs Control condition (4.16) average trust scores](images/ai-trust-bar.png) **95% Tukey Confidence Interval** ![Tukey confidence interval plot showing the difference in means crosses zero, indicating no significant difference](images/tukey-ci.png) **Summary Statistics Table** | Statistic | Value | |----------------------|--------------------------------------------------| | Mean Trust (AI) | 4.24 | | Mean Trust (Control) | 4.16 | | Mean Difference | 0.08 | | F-Statistic | 0.745 | | p-Value | 0.39 | | 95% CI (Tukey) | \[-0.280, 0.110\] | | Tukey p-Value | 0.39 | | Interpretation | No statistically significant difference in trust | **Trust Item Breakdown by Condition** ![Horizontal bar chart comparing AI vs Control scores across all 10 trust survey items](images/trust-breakdown.png%20.png) ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ### 💡 Insights - Both AI and control emails generated **high trust scores** (above 4.0 on a 5-point scale), suggesting the email content itself was well-received regardless of personalization. - The AI condition scored higher on **data comfort** and **accuracy/reliability**, while the control scored slightly higher on **privacy respect** and **authenticity**. - The lack of statistical significance may reflect a **ceiling effect** — participants rated both emails highly, leaving little room to detect meaningful differences. ## Future research could explore trust differences with **larger, more diverse samples** or test emails with stronger AI personalization cues. ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## MEin3D — AI-Powered Email Personalization (Graduate Capstone)

AO5 — Website Funnel Analysis *Kathleen Juarez* :::::: columns ::: {.column width="50%"} ```{r ao5-funnel, fig.height=4.2} tibble( stage=factor(c("Sessions","Page Views","Form Start","Bookings"), levels=c("Sessions","Page Views","Form Start","Bookings")), n=c(563,470,38,5), fill=c("a","a","b","c") ) |> ggplot(aes(x=stage, y=n, fill=fill)) + geom_bar(stat="identity", width=0.6) + geom_text(aes(label=n), vjust=-0.4, fontface="bold", size=7) + scale_fill_manual(values=c("a"="#1a3a5c","b"="#c0392b","c"="#27ae60")) + scale_y_continuous(limits=c(0,640)) + labs(title="Booking Funnel — Jan 1 to Apr 21, 2026", x=NULL, y="Users") + theme_minimal(base_size=14) + theme(legend.position="none") ``` ::: :::: {.column width="50%"} | Channel | Sessions | Conv. Rate | |----------------|----------|------------| | Unassigned | 32 | **3.1%** | | Organic Search | 86 | **2.3%** | | Organic Social | 207 | 0.5% | | Direct | 213 | 0.5% | | **TOTAL** | **563** | **0.9%** | ::: red-box **99.1% of visitors leave without booking.** Sharpest drop at Form Start. Organic Search converts 4× better than Social. ::: :::: :::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## Recommendations & Summary :::::::::::: columns ::::::::: {.column width="50%"} **Immediate (0–30 days)** ::: rec-item Deploy AI-personalized CTAs — 2.6× CTR, trust preserved ::: ::: rec-item Fix the booking form — 92% drop-off at Form Start ::: **Medium-Term (30–90 days)** ::: rec-item Invest in Organic Search — highest converting channel ::: ::: rec-item Scale A/B testing to n ≈ 600+ to confirm AO4 ::: **Long-Term (90+ days)** ::: rec-item Build automated email nurture sequences ::: ::: rec-item Implement GA4 purchase event tracking ::: ::::::::: :::: {.column width="50%"} | AO | Key Finding | |-----|-------------------------------------------| | AO1 | 10% CTOR baseline — content is bottleneck | | AO2 | AI preserves trust (p = 0.39) | | AO3 | 5% click vs. 0% generic | | AO4 | **2× CTOR, 2.6× CTR** lift | | AO5 | 99.1% don't book — fix funnel | ::: big-quote The trust is there. Fix the funnel. ::: :::: :::::::::::: ------------------------------------------------------------------------ ## Thank You ::: thank-you-box **Group 2 — IBM 6530** Nerina Campos · Jessica Garcia · Kathleen Juarez · Krystal Lee · **Serena Shi (Project Lead)** *Faculty Lead: Dr. Elif Ozkaya* GitHub: [github.com/soserena/mein3d_project](https://github.com/soserena/mein3d_project) :::
